expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: donut-chart data label coverage distinguishes label visibility, density, and overlap-sensitive outcomes
expectations.evidence: The output does not provide Phase 4a plan content covering donut label visibility/density/overlap behaviors; it explicitly states it cannot be demonstrated: “Not satisfied (insufficient evidence to demonstrate ... the donut-label focus coverage).” and “Without a Phase 4a draft or richer behavioral requirements, the benchmark’s focus cannot be shown as ‘explicitly covered.’”

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4a
expectations.evidence: No Phase 4a artifact is present and the result says alignment cannot be demonstrated: “there is no Phase 4a draft artifact to review ... Therefore, Phase 4a alignment cannot be demonstrated.” The produced file is a benchmark verdict, not a Phase 4a subcategory-only QA draft.
